marker.regression_cause: vLLM dev307 (vllm-0.22.1rc1.dev307+g3e8afdf78) drops the Gemma4 Per-Layer-Embedding embed_scale (sqrt(256)=16.0) on the plain serve path: gemma4.py::get_per_layer_inputs no longer multiplies the per-layer embeddings by embed_scale_per_layer at runtime (the 'Challenge fast path'), and the scale is instead applied by a LOAD-TIME fold gated behind env PLE_FOLD_EMBED_SCALE=1 (model_loader/utils.py:130). A vanilla serve never sets that env var, so every decoder layer receives 16x-too-small per-layer embeddings -> subtly corrupted long-CoT greedy decode -> repetition loops -> max_tokens truncation (36.6%/33.8% MMLU/GPQA) -> 0.432/0.3131. Setting PLE_FOLD_EMBED_SCALE=1 on the SAME plain vanilla serve (no MTP, no 2D attn, no split-KV, no onegraph) folds the scale into the embedding weights ('Folded Gemma4 PLE embed scale 16.0 into weight') and recovers 0.662/0.485 with truncation collapsed to 9.6%/16.2%. The 2D order-preserving attention path alone (surgical_attn, no fold) does NOT fix it (0.414/0.242, trunc 37.6%/41.9%) — the attention backend is NOT the cause. A stock global FLASH_ATTN override (VLLM_ATTENTION_BACKEND=FLASH_ATTN) does NOT take effect: dev307's config.py:100 force-selects TRITON_ATTN for the heterogeneous head dims regardless (server comes up on TRITON, load_failed=False). Attention is thus held CONSTANT (forced TRITON_ATTN) across the broken base, surgical_attn, and ple_fold arms — the PLE embed-scale fold is the sole differentiator.

global_fa.reason: server reached /v1/models READY under global FLASH_ATTN (unexpected)
